REPLY MEMO P/A IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT HERSH & HERSH, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS COMPLAINANT HERSH & HERSH'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $18,150 IN ATTORNEYS FEES AND $90 IN COSTS UNDER CCP 425.16(C)(2), WHICH IS THE AMOUNT REASONABLY EXPENDED ON THE SUCCESSFUL PORTION OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED AS TO COUNSEL'S EXPERIENCE AND ABILITY, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL'S HOURLY RATE OF $550 IS REASONABLE. THE DECLARATION OF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL SUBSTANTIATES THE HOURS EXPENDED AND THE NATURE OF THE WORK DONE ON THE MOTION (35.9 HOURS), AND FURTHER ESTABLISHES THAT THESE HOURS DO NOT INCLUDE TIME SPENT ON THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION OR ANY OTHER MATTERS IN THE CASE. ALTHOUGH THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION DID NOT RESULT IN THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION BEING DISMISSED, THE MOTION WAS ALMOST ENTIRELY SUCCESSFUL IN ACHIEVING DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIVE OF DISMISSING THE TORT CAUSES OF ACTION AND THE DEFAMATION ALLEGATIONS. THE COURT REDUCES THE 35.9 HOURS BY 2.9 HOURS (FOR A TOTAL OF 33.0 HOURS) TO TAKE INTO ACCOUNT THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT ENTIRELY SUCCESSFUL. THE COURT FINDS THAT RELATIVELY LITTLE EFFORT AND TIME WAS EXPENDED EXCLUSIVELY ON THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, WHICH WERE NOT DISMISSED. THE REMAINING TIME (33 HOURS) WAS FOR WORK FOCUSED ON THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THIS TIME IS REASONABLE CONSIDERING THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE OVERALL RELIEF IN RELATION TO THE HOURS WORKED. SEE MANN V. QUALITY OLD TIME SERVICES, INC. (2006) 139 CAL. APP. 4TH 328, 344-45. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS IN SUPPORT OF THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WERE PARTICULARLY COGENT AND TO THE POINT. AN ADDITIONAL ATTORNEY'S FEE IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,950.00 FOR 9 HOURS SPENT IN PREPARING THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS HERSH & HERSH, NANCY HERSH & MARK BURTON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE SECOND, THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. ON THE FACTS OF THIS CASE, THE COMMERCIAL SPEECH EXCEPTION IN CCP 425.17 CLEARLY DOES NOT APPLY. (SEE TAHERI LAW GROUP V. EVANS (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 482, 489-492.) PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING BECAUSE THE CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. (SEE CAL. CIV. CODE 47(B).) THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY BECAUSE THE GRAVAMEN OF THOSE CLAIMS IS NOT THE PROTECTED SPEECH. PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Notice O Fmotion And Moiton By Defts For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JACK PRAETZELLIS OF THE MBV LAW LLP, TEL. NO. 781-4400 EXT. 1424 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ARTHUR D. LEVY, TEL. NO. 702-4550 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS COMPLAINANT HERSH & HERSH's motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $18,150 in attorneys fees and $90 in costs under CCP 425.16(c)(2), which is the amount reasonably expended on the successful portion of Defendant's motion to strike. Based on the evidence presented as to counsel's experience and ability, Defendant's counsel's hourly rate of $550 is reasonable. The declaration of Defendant's counsel substantiates the hours expended and the nature of the work done on the motion (35.9 hours), and further establishes that these hours do not include time spent on the motion for preliminary injunction or any other matters in the case. Although the anti-SLAPP motion did not result in the first and fourth causes of action being dismissed, the motion was almost entirely successful in achieving Defendant's objective of dismissing the tort causes of action and the defamation allegations. The Court reduces the 35.9 hours by 2.9 hours (for a total of 33.0 hours) to take into account that the motion was not entirely successful. The Court finds that relatively little effort and time was expended exclusively on the first and fourth causes of action, which were not dismissed. The remaining time (33 hours) was for work focused on the other causes of action. This time is reasonable considering the significance of the overall relief in relation to the hours worked. See Mann v. Quality Old Time Services, Inc. (2006) 139 Cal. App. 4th 328, 344-45. The Court finds that the moving and reply papers in support of the anti-SLAPP motion were particularly cogent and to the point. An additional attorney's fee is granted in the amount of $4,950.00 for 9 hours spent in preparing this motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

ARTHUR D. LEVY, ESQ., 415.702.4550, FOR DEFENDANT HERSH & HERSH.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling REGARDING PLAINTIFF LEVIN SIMES, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP'S Application For Preliminary Injunction.
The Court has reviewed the Defendants' status report filed April 3, 2013. The application for preliminary injunction is denied as moot. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Arthur D. Levy, Esq. of the Law Office of Arthur Levy, Tel. 415.702.4550, for defendants and moving party;
Jonathan O'Donnell, Esq. of MBV Law, Tel. 415.781.4400, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs HERSH & HERSH, NANCY HERSH & MARK BURTON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted as to the second, third and fifth causes of action and denied as to the first and fourth causes of action. The second, third and fifth causes of action arise from protected activity. On the facts of this case, the commercial speech exception in CCP 425.17 clearly does not apply. (See Taheri Law Group v. Evans (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 482, 489-492.) Plaintiff has failed to show a probability of prevailing because the claims are barred by the litigation privilege. (See Cal. Civ. Code 47(b).) The first and fourth causes of action do not arise from protected activity because the gravamen of those claims is not the protected speech. Plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
